# DDCS M350 G-Code Syntax Reference for Verifier

**Purpose**: Complete syntax rules for validating G-code generated by DDCS Studio  
**Controller**: DDCS Expert M350 V1.22  
**Generators**: Corner, Middle, Edge, Communication, WCS

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Critical Syntax Rules](#1-critical-syntax-rules)
2. [Motion Commands](#2-motion-commands)
3. [Variables](#3-variables)
4. [Conditionals & Flow Control](#4-conditionals--flow-control)
5. [G31 Probe Commands](#5-g31-probe-commands)
6. [WCS Management](#6-wcs-management)
7. [Communication & Display](#7-communication--display)
8. [M-Codes](#8-m-codes)
9. [Command Deck Reference](#9-command-deck-reference)
10. [Validation Patterns](#10-validation-patterns)

---

## 1. Critical Syntax Rules

### 1.1 The Eight Core Truths

| # | Truth | ❌ Invalid | ✅ Valid |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | No `AXIS+#VAR` in motion (positive sign before var is disallowed) | `G31 Y+#1` | `G31 Y#8`, `G0 X-#2` |
| 2 | No brackets on simple IF | `IF [#1!=2] GOTO1` | `IF #1!=2 GOTO1` |
| 3 | No space before GOTO label | `GOTO 1` | `GOTO1` |
| 4 | C-style operators only | `IF #1 EQ 5` | `IF #1==5` |
| 5 | G10 is broken | `G10 L2 P1 X0 Y0 Z0` | `#805=#880` |
| 6 | G53 needs variables | `G53 X0 Y0 Z0` | `G53 X#x Y#y Z#z` |
| 7 | Persistent vars need priming | `#1153=#880` | `#100=#880` then `#1153=#100` |
| 8 | WCS stride is 5 (not 20) | `805 + wcs*20` | `805 + [wcs-1]*5` |

### 1.2 Motion Arithmetic - What Actually Works

**Based on community macros (macro_cam10.nc, macro_cam12.nc, etc.):**

| Pattern | Status | Example |
|---------|--------|---------|
| `AXIS-#VAR` | ✅ Works | `G31 Z-#1`, `G0 X-#2` |
| `AXIS#VAR` | ✅ Works | `G31 Y#8`, `G0 Z#10` |
| `AXIS[expr]` | ✅ Works | `G53 Z[#20+#15]`, `G0 Y[#15*2]` |
| `AXIS+#VAR` | ❌ Not used | No community examples found |

**Verified working patterns from production macros:**
```gcode
G91 G31 Z-#1 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1    ( macro_cam10.nc )
G91 G31 Y-#2 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1    ( macro_cam11.nc )
G91 G31 X-#1 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1    ( macro_cam12.nc )
G53 Z[#20+#15]                  ( macro_cam10.nc )
G91 G31 Y[#15*2] F#14 P#16 L0 Q1  ( macro_cam12.nc )
```

### 1.3 Pre-Calculated Motion Variables Pattern (Optional)

While `AXIS-#VAR` works, pre-calculated variables provide cleaner code:

```gcode
( Base parameters - user inputs )
#1=60       ( Max probe distance )
#2=5        ( Retract distance )
#3=200      ( Fast feed )
#4=50       ( Slow feed )
#5=3        ( Probe port )
#6=2        ( Clearance distance )

( Pre-calculated motion values )
#7=[0-#1]       ( Negative max probe )
#8=#1           ( Positive max probe )
#9=[0-#2]       ( Negative retract )
#10=#2          ( Positive retract )
#11=[0-#2*2]    ( Negative double retract )
#12=[#2*2]      ( Positive double retract )
#13=#6          ( Positive clearance )
#14=[0-#6]      ( Negative clearance )
#15=50          ( Positive travel distance )
#16=[0-50]      ( Negative travel distance )
#17=15          ( Plunge depth )
#18=[0-#17]     ( Negative plunge )
#19=10          ( Safe Z height )

( Motion commands reference pre-calculated variables )
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1      ( Probe Z negative ✅ )
G0 Z#10                     ( Retract Z positive ✅ )
G31 Y#8 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1      ( Probe Y positive ✅ )
G0 Y#9                      ( Retract Y negative ✅ )
```

---

## 2. Motion Commands

### 2.1 Supported G-Codes

| Code | Function | Parameters |
|------|----------|------------|
| `G0` | Rapid positioning | `X Y Z A B` |
| `G1` | Linear interpolation | `X Y Z A B F` |
| `G4` | Dwell | `P` (milliseconds) |
| `G31` | Probe move | `X/Y/Z F P L Q` |
| `G53` | Machine coordinates | `X Y Z` (variables only) |
| `G90` | Absolute mode | (none) |
| `G91` | Incremental mode | (none) |

### 2.2 Valid Motion Syntax

```gcode
( Hardcoded values )
G0 X100 Y200 Z50                    ✅
G1 X50 F500                         ✅
G0 Z-15                             ✅

( Variable references )
G0 X#100 Y#101 Z#102                ✅
G1 Z#depth F#feed                   ✅
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1               ✅

( Negative sign before variable - VERIFIED WORKING )
G91 G31 Z-#1 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1         ✅ (macro_cam10.nc)
G91 G31 Y-#2 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1         ✅ (macro_cam11.nc)
G91 G31 X-#1 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1         ✅ (macro_cam12.nc)
G0 Z-#190                           ✅ (macro_Thread_milling.nc)
G1 Z-#18 F#28                       ✅ (macro_Adaptive_Pocket.nc)

( Expressions in brackets )
G53 Z[#20+#15]                      ✅ (macro_cam10.nc)
G53 Z[#20+10]                       ✅ (macro_cam10.nc)
G91 G31 Y[#15*2] F#14 P#16 L0 Q1    ✅ (macro_cam12.nc)
G91 G31 Y[#15*-2] F#14 P#16 L0 Q1   ✅ (macro_cam12.nc)
G0 X[#100+50]                       ✅
```

### 2.3 Invalid/Untested Motion Syntax

```gcode
( Positive sign before variable - NO community examples found! )
G0 X+#100                           ⚠️ UNTESTED - avoid
G31 Y+#1                            ⚠️ UNTESTED - avoid

( G53 with hardcoded constants )
G53 X0 Y0 Z0                        ❌ FAILS

( G53 combined with G0/G1 on same line )
G53 G0 X#var                        ❌ FAILS
```

### 2.4 Axis Letters

```
X - X axis
Y - Y axis
Z - Z axis
A - 4th axis (slave Y on dual-gantry)
B - 5th axis
F - Feedrate (mm/min)
```

---

## 3. Variables

### 3.1 Variable Assignment Syntax

```gcode
( Direct assignment )
#1=60                               ✅
#var=100                            ❌ (named variables not supported)

( Expressions )
#7=[0-#1]                           ✅ (negative of #1)
#8=#1                               ✅ (copy)
#10=[#2*2]                          ✅ (arithmetic)
#base=805+[#wcs-1]*5                ✅ (calculation)

( Indirect addressing )
#[#70+0]=#1925                      ✅ (write to calculated address)
#[#70+2]=#1927                      ✅
#value=#[805+#offset]               ✅ (read from calculated address)

( Priming pattern - required for persistent vars )
#100=#880                           ✅ (prime to local first)
#1153=#100                          ✅ (then assign to persistent)
#1153=#880+0                        ✅ (arithmetic wash alternative)
```

### 3.2 Variable Ranges

| Range | Purpose | Priming | Notes |
|-------|---------|---------|-------|
| #1-#49 | User local | No | Safe for any use |
| #50-#499 | User temporary | No | #2070 can write here |
| #500-#999 | Parameter mirrors | No | Read Pr0-Pr499 |
| #805-#834 | WCS offsets | No | G54-G59 X/Y/Z/A/B |
| #880-#884 | Machine position | N/A | Read-only |
| #1153-#5999 | Persistent storage | **YES** | Survives power cycle |
| #1500-#1513 | Display system | No | See Communication section |
| #1920-#1929 | Probe results | N/A | Read-only |

### 3.3 Key System Variables

```gcode
( Machine position - read only )
#880    ( Machine X position )
#881    ( Machine Y position )
#882    ( Machine Z position )
#883    ( Machine A position )
#884    ( Machine B position )

( Active WCS )
#578    ( Active WCS: 1=G54, 2=G55, ... 6=G59 )

( Probe results - read only )
#1920   ( X probe status: 0=none, 1=init, 2=success, 3=neg limit, 4=pos limit )
#1921   ( Y probe status )
#1922   ( Z probe status )
#1925   ( X trigger position - machine coords )
#1926   ( Y trigger position )
#1927   ( Z trigger position )

( Probe configuration )
#629    ( Pr129: Probe/puck thickness )
```

---

## 4. Conditionals & Flow Control

### 4.1 IF Statement Syntax

**Simple conditions (NO brackets):**
```gcode
IF #1922!=2 GOTO1                   ✅
IF #var==5 GOTO2                    ✅
IF #100<50 GOTO99                   ✅
IF #temp>=0 GOTO1                   ✅
IF #1921==1 GOTO1                   ✅
```

**Complex expressions (brackets required):**
```gcode
IF [#100+#200]>50 GOTO2             ✅
IF [#a*#b]!=0 GOTO1                 ✅
IF [#14==5]+[#14==4] GOTO10         ✅ (boolean OR)
```

**Invalid patterns:**
```gcode
IF [#1922!=2] GOTO1                 ❌ (brackets on simple condition)
IF #1922 != 2 GOTO 1                ❌ (spaces everywhere)
IF #1922 NE 2 GOTO1                 ❌ (FANUC-style operator)
IF[#1922!=2]GOTO1                   ❌ (no space after IF)
```

### 4.2 Comparison Operators

| Use This | Not This | Meaning |
|----------|----------|---------|
| `==` | `EQ` | Equal |
| `!=` | `NE` | Not equal |
| `<` | `LT` | Less than |
| `>` | `GT` | Greater than |
| `<=` | `LE` | Less or equal |
| `>=` | `GE` | Greater or equal |

### 4.3 GOTO Syntax

```gcode
GOTO1                               ✅
GOTO2                               ✅
GOTO99                              ✅

GOTO 1                              ❌ (space before label)
GO TO1                              ❌ (split keyword)
```

### 4.4 Label Syntax

```gcode
( Preferred - single digit, most reliable )
N1                                  ✅✅
N2                                  ✅✅
N9                                  ✅✅

( Acceptable - double digit )
N10                                 ✅
N99                                 ✅

( Use with caution - may cause "label not found" )
N100                                ⚠️
N999                                ⚠️
```

### 4.5 Standard Program Flow Pattern

```gcode
( Header )
( Program description )

( Variables )
#1=60
#2=5

( Pre-calculated motion vars )
#7=[0-#1]
#8=#1

( Main execution )
G91
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
IF #1922!=2 GOTO1              ( Jump to error on failure )

( More operations... )

( Success path )
G90
#1505=-5000(Success!)
GOTO2                          ( MUST jump past error handler )

( Error handler )
N1
#1505=1(Failed!)
G90

( Program end )
N2
M30
```

### 4.6 WHILE Loop Syntax

```gcode
WHILE #counter<10 DO1
    ( loop body )
    #counter=#counter+1
END1
```

---

## 5. G31 Probe Commands

### 5.1 Full Syntax

```
G31 <axis><distance> F<feed> P<port> L<level> Q<stop>
```

| Parameter | Description | Values |
|-----------|-------------|--------|
| axis | X, Y, Z, A, or B | Single axis per command |
| distance | Probe distance | Variable (#7) or value (-50) |
| F | Feedrate | mm/min |
| P | Probe input port | 1-16 typically |
| L | Signal level | 0=NO (normally open), 1=NC |
| Q | Stop mode | 0=decelerate, 1=immediate |

### 5.2 Valid G31 Patterns

```gcode
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1               ✅ (all variables)
G31 Z-50 F200 P3 L0 Q1              ✅ (hardcoded)
G31 Y#8 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1               ✅
G31 X#7 F200 P3 L0 Q0               ✅
```

### 5.3 Invalid G31 Patterns

```gcode
G31 Z+#1 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1              ❌ (positive sign before variable disallowed)
G31 Z-#1 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1              ✅ (negative signed variable accepted)
G31 Y+#2 F#4 P#5 L0 Q1              ❌ (positive sign before variable disallowed)
G31 Y-#2 F#4 P#5 L0 Q1              ✅ (negative signed variable accepted)
```

### 5.4 Probe Result Checking Pattern

```gcode
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
IF #1922!=2 GOTO1                   ( Check Z probe status )

G31 Y#8 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
IF #1921!=2 GOTO1                   ( Check Y probe status )

G31 X#8 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
IF #1920!=2 GOTO1                   ( Check X probe status )

( Probe status values:
   0 = No probe executed
   1 = Initializing
   2 = SUCCESS - probe triggered
   3 = Hit negative limit
   4 = Hit positive limit
)
```

### 5.5 Two-Pass Probe Pattern

```gcode
( Fast approach )
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
IF #1922!=2 GOTO1
G0 Z#10                             ( Retract )

( Slow precision probe )
G31 Z#11 F#4 P#5 L0 Q1              ( #11 = negative double retract )
IF #1922!=2 GOTO1

( Save result )
#[#70+2]=#1927                      ( Save Z to WCS )
```

---

## 6. WCS Management

### 6.1 WCS Address Map

| WCS | Base | X | Y | Z | A | B |
|-----|------|---|---|---|---|---|
| G54 | 805 | #805 | #806 | #807 | #808 | #809 |
| G55 | 810 | #810 | #811 | #812 | #813 | #814 |
| G56 | 815 | #815 | #816 | #817 | #818 | #819 |
| G57 | 820 | #820 | #821 | #822 | #823 | #824 |
| G58 | 825 | #825 | #826 | #827 | #828 | #829 |
| G59 | 830 | #830 | #831 | #832 | #833 | #834 |

**Formula**: `Address = 805 + (WCS_Index - 1) * 5 + Axis_Offset`  
**Axis Offset**: X=0, Y=1, Z=2, A=3, B=4

### 6.2 Direct WCS Writing (Correct Method)

```gcode
( Set G54 to current machine position )
#805=#880                           ( G54 X )
#806=#881                           ( G54 Y )
#807=#882                           ( G54 Z )

( Set G57 Z from probe result )
#822=#1927                          ( G57 Z = probe trigger position )
```

### 6.3 Dynamic WCS Calculation

```gcode
( Read active WCS and calculate base address )
#71=1                               ( Wash/prime )
#71=#578                            ( Read active WCS: 1-6 )
#70=805+[#71-1]*5                   ( Calculate base address )

( Write offsets using indirect addressing )
#[#70+0]=#1925                      ( Set X offset )
#[#70+1]=#1926                      ( Set Y offset )
#[#70+2]=#1927                      ( Set Z offset )
```

### 6.4 Fixed WCS Selection

```gcode
( Target specific WCS )
#70=805                             ( G54 )
#70=810                             ( G55 )
#70=815                             ( G56 )
#70=820                             ( G57 )
#70=825                             ( G58 )
#70=830                             ( G59 )
```

### 6.5 WCS Wizard Patterns

```gcode
( Auto-detect active WCS )
#150=#578
#151=805+[#150-1]*5

( Zero selected axes )
#[#151+0]=#880                      ( X )
#[#151+1]=#881                      ( Y )
#[#151+2]=#882                      ( Z )
#[#151+3]=#883                      ( A )

( Dual gantry sync )
#[#151+3]=#883                      ( Sync A with Y )
```

---

## 7. Communication & Display

### 7.1 Display Variables

| Variable | Function | Max Chars |
|----------|----------|-----------|
| #1503 | Status bar message | 68 |
| #1505 | Popup dialog | 68 |
| #1510-#1513 | Data slots for formatting | N/A |
| #2070 | Numeric input dialog | N/A |
| #2042 | System beep | N/A |
| #3000 | Alarm trigger | N/A |

### 7.2 Status Bar (#1503)

```gcode
( Simple message )
#1503=1(Probing in progress...)

( Message with variables - use #1510-#1513 )
#1510=#result
#1511=#position
#1503=1(Result: [%.3f] Position: [%.1f])
```

### 7.3 Popup Dialog (#1505)

**Line breaks**: use `/` inside message text to break lines on controller popups.

```gcode
#1505=1(Hello! First row /second row /third row)
```

```gcode
( Simple confirmation - waits for CYCLE START )
#1505=1(Press CYCLE START to begin)
M0

( Success message - negative values don't pause )
#1505=-5000(Operation complete!)

( Error message )
#1505=1(Probe failed - no contact!)

( With data slots )
#1510=#measured_value
#1505=1(Measured: [%.3f] mm)
M0
```

### 7.4 Popup Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| 1 | Standard popup, wait for input |
| 3 | Binary choice (Enter/Esc) |
| -5000 | Success message (no wait) |

**Controller note**: `#0245=line` enables controller simulation mode; useful operationally, but not part of macro message syntax.

### 7.5 Numeric Input (#2070)

**CRITICAL**: #2070 can only write to #50-#499 range!

```gcode
( WRONG - silent failure )
#2070=1175(Enter speed...)              ❌

( CORRECT - two-step pattern )
#2070=100(Enter probe speed mm/min)     ✅ (writes to #100)
#1175=#100                              ( Copy to persistent )
```

### 7.6 System Beep (#2042)

```gcode
#2042=1                                 ( Single beep )
#2042=3                                 ( Triple beep )
```

### 7.7 Alarm (#3000)

```gcode
#3000=1(ALARM: Probe failed!)           ( Triggers alarm state )
```

### 7.8 Dwell (G4)

```gcode
G4 P1000                                ( Pause 1000ms = 1 second )
G4 P5000                                ( Pause 5 seconds )
G4 P#delay                              ( Variable delay )
```

### 7.9 Program Stop (M0)

```gcode
#1505=1(Position probe and press CYCLE START)
M0                                      ( Waits for operator )
```

### 7.10 Communication Wizard Patterns

```gcode
( Popup with message )
#1505=1(Your message here)
M0

( Status bar update )
#1503=1(Status message)

( Numeric input with persistent storage )
#2070=105(Enter value)
#1175=#105

( Beep )
#2042=1

( Alarm )
#3000=1(MSG,Alarm message)

( Dwell )
G4 P5000

( Key wait )
#2038=0
M0
```

---

## 8. M-Codes

### 8.1 Supported M-Codes

| Code | Function | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| M0 | Program stop | Waits for CYCLE START |
| M3 | Spindle CW | With S for speed |
| M4 | Spindle CCW | With S for speed |
| M5 | Spindle off | |
| M8 | Coolant on | |
| M9 | Coolant off | |
| M30 | Program end | Reset and rewind |
| M98 | Call subprogram | M98 P<number> |
| M99 | Return from sub | |

### 8.2 M30 Warning

M30 can be glitchy in complex macros. Use GOTO to final label pattern:

```gcode
( Success )
GOTO2

N1
( Error handler )

N2
M30
```

---

## 9. Command Deck Reference

### 9.1 Generators

| Button | Wizard | Purpose |
|--------|--------|---------|
| 📐 Corner | cornerWizard | Find workpiece corners (FL, FR, BL, BR) |
| 🎯 Middle | middleWizard | Find center of pockets/bosses |
| 📏 Edge | edgeWizard | Find single or both edges |
| 💬 Comm | communicationWizard | Popups, status, input, beeps |
| 🔧 WCS | wcsWizard | Zero work coordinate systems |

### 9.2 M-Codes & Hardware

| Button | Inserts | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| M3 | `M3 ` | Spindle on CW |
| M5 | `M5 ` | Spindle off |
| M8 | `M8 ` | Coolant on |
| M9 | `M9 ` | Coolant off |

### 9.3 G-Codes

| Button | Inserts | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| G0 | `G0 ` | Rapid move |
| G1 | `G1 ` | Linear move |
| G53 | `G53 ` | Machine coordinates |
| G31 | `G31 ` | Probe move |
| G90 | `G90 ` | Absolute mode |
| G91 | `G91 ` | Incremental mode |
| G4 | `G4 P` | Dwell |
| F | `F` | Feedrate |

### 9.4 Axes & Addresses

| Button | Inserts | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| X | `X` | X axis |
| Y | `Y` | Y axis |
| Z | `Z` | Z axis |
| A | `A` | A axis |
| B | `B` | B axis |
| # | `#` | Variable prefix |

### 9.5 Snippets

| Button | Inserts | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| M30 | `M30` | Program end |
| SafeZ | `safe_z` | Safe Z snippet |
| WASH | `=1` | Variable priming wash |

---

## 10. Validation Patterns

### 10.1 Critical Errors (Must Fix)

```javascript
// Invalid inline arithmetic in motion commands
/[XYZAB][+-]#\d+/g
// Matches: Y+#1, X-#2, Z+#1, Y-#2

// Bracketed simple IF conditions
/IF\s*\[\s*#\d+\s*[!=<>]=?\s*\d+\s*\]\s*GOTO/gi
// Matches: IF [#1922!=2] GOTO1

// Space before GOTO label
/GOTO\s+\d+/gi
// Matches: GOTO 1, GOTO 999
```

### 10.2 Warnings (Should Fix)

```javascript
// FANUC-style operators
/\b(EQ|NE|LT|GT|LE|GE)\b/g
// Matches: EQ, NE, LT, GT, LE, GE

// High label numbers (may be unreliable)
/N[1-9]\d{2,}/g
// Matches: N100, N999, N1000

// G10 usage (broken on DDCS)
/G10\s+L/gi
// Matches: G10 L2

// G53 with hardcoded values
/G53\s+[XYZAB]0(?!\d)/gi
// Matches: G53 X0 Y0 Z0
```

### 10.3 Valid Pattern Examples

```gcode
( ✅ All these patterns are valid )

( Motion with pre-calculated variables )
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
G0 Z#10
G31 Y#8 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1
G0 Y#9

( Simple IF without brackets )
IF #1922!=2 GOTO1
IF #1921!=2 GOTO1
IF #1920!=2 GOTO1

( GOTO without space )
GOTO1
GOTO2

( Variable assignment )
#7=[0-#1]
#8=#1
#[#70+2]=#1927

( WCS management )
#70=820
#[#70+0]=#1925
#[#70+1]=#1926
#[#70+2]=#1927

( Communication )
#1505=1(Message)
M0
#1503=1(Status)
#1505=-5000(Success!)
```

---

## 11. Comments

### 11.1 Valid Comment Styles

```gcode
( This is a comment )               ✅ Parentheses
; This is a comment                 ✅ Semicolon
// This is a comment                ✅ Double slash
```

### 11.2 Inline Comments

```gcode
G0 X100 ( move to start )           ✅
#1=60 ( Max probe distance )        ✅
G31 Z#7 F#3 P#5 L0 Q1 ( Fast probe ) ✅
```

---

## 12. Community Macro Examples (Verified Working Code)

These are actual snippets from production macros tested on DDCS M350 controllers.

### 12.1 macro_cam10.nc - Z Surface Probe

```gcode
(Find surface with 3D touch probe)

#1=#2600  ;max stroke Z
#2=#2601  ;Probe length
#3=200  ;fast speed

#14=20    ;slow speed
#15=1     ;retract
#16=3  ;input port number of the probe (YunKia V6 on IN03)
#17=[#2602-54]*5; Working coordinate system offset

#10=#882  ;current Z machine position
#20=0  ;Z position

//Z Axis.
G91 G31 Z-#1 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1  ;probe down (AXIS-#VAR works!)
IF #1921==1 GOTO1  ;edge not found!
#20=#1927  ;store position
G53 Z[#20+#15]   ;retract (bracket expression works!)
G91 G31 Z[-#15*2] F#14 P#16 L0 Q1  ;slow probe (bracket expression)
IF #1922==1 GOTO1  ;edge not found!
#20=#1927
G53 Z[#20+10]   ;retract 10mm

#[807+#17]=#20-#2  ;save to WCS (indirect addressing)
#1505=1(Surface found!)
GOTO2  ;end

N1
#1505=1(Edge not found!)

N2
M30  ;end
```

**Key patterns demonstrated:**
- `G91 G31 Z-#1` - Negative sign before variable ✅
- `G53 Z[#20+#15]` - Bracket expression with addition ✅
- `G91 G31 Z[-#15*2]` - Bracket with multiplication ✅
- `#[807+#17]=` - Indirect addressing for WCS ✅
- `IF #1921==1 GOTO1` - Simple IF without brackets ✅

### 12.2 macro_cam12.nc - Edge Center Finding

```gcode
(Find outer edge center with 3D touch probe)

#1=#2608  ;max movement X
#2=#2609  ;max movement Y
#3=#2610  ;fast speed
#5=#2611  ; scanning depth Z

#14=20    ;slow speed
#15=1     ;retract
#16=3  ;input port number of the probe
#17=[#2615-54]*5; Working coordinate system offset

#10=#880  ;current X machine position
#11=#881  ;current Y machine position
#12=#882  ;current Z machine position

#20=0  ;left position
#21=0  ;right position
#22=0  ;back position
#23=0  ;front position

//Y Axis. 
IF #7==0 GOTO4    ;skip if Y disabled

G53 Z#12          ;safe Z
G91 G31 Y-#2 F[#3*3] P#16 L0 Q1  ;move back (Y-#VAR + F[expr])
IF #1921!=1 GOTO3  ;Collision

G91 G31 Z#5 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1  ;Scanning height
IF #1922!=1 GOTO3  ;Collision

G91 G31 Y#2 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1  ;fast scan forward
IF #1921==1 GOTO1  ;edge not found!
#22=#1926  ;edge found

G53 Y[#22-#15]   ;retract (bracket subtraction)
G91 G31 Y[#15*2] F#14 P#16 L0 Q1  ;Slow scan
IF #1921==1 GOTO1  ;edge not found!

#22=#1926 ; Edge found
G53 Z#12  ; safe Z
G53 Y#11  ;returns to the starting point

G91 G31 Y-#2 F#3 P#16 L0 Q1  ;fast scan back
IF #1921==1 GOTO1  ;edge not found!
#23=#1926  ;edge found

G53 Y[#23+#15]   ;retract
G91 G31 Y[#15*-2] F#14 P#16 L0 Q1  ;Slow scan (negative multiplier!)
IF #1921==1 GOTO1  ;edge not found!
#23=#1926 ; Edge found

#25=[#22+#23]/2  ;calc center Y
G53 Z#12    ;safe Z
G53 Y#25  ;move to center Y
```

**Key patterns demonstrated:**
- `G91 G31 Y-#2` - Negative variable works ✅
- `F[#3*3]` - Feedrate can use bracket expressions ✅
- `G53 Y[#22-#15]` - G53 with bracket expression ✅
- `G91 G31 Y[#15*-2]` - Negative multiplier in brackets ✅
- `#25=[#22+#23]/2` - Division in variable assignment ✅

### 12.3 macro_DA_without_relay_advanced.nc - Multi-Axis Probe

```gcode
;; Complex multi-axis probe move (line 158)
G91G31X-#71Y-#72Z-#73A-#74B-#75C-#76 F#563 P#19 L1 K0 Q0

;; Same pattern repeated (line 265)
N209 G91G31X-#71Y-#72Z-#73A-#74B-#75C-#76 F#563 P#45 L1 K0 Q0
```

**Key pattern demonstrated:**
- Multiple axes with negative variables on single line ✅
- All `AXIS-#VAR` patterns work together ✅

### 12.4 Summary of Verified Motion Patterns

| Pattern | Source | Line | Status |
|---------|--------|------|--------|
| `G31 Z-#1` | macro_cam10.nc | 16 | ✅ Works |
| `G53 Z[#20+#15]` | macro_cam10.nc | 19 | ✅ Works |
| `G31 Z[-#15*2]` | macro_cam10.nc | 20 | ✅ Works |
| `G31 Y-#2` | macro_cam12.nc | 34 | ✅ Works |
| `F[#3*3]` | macro_cam12.nc | 34 | ✅ Works |
| `G53 Y[#22-#15]` | macro_cam12.nc | 44 | ✅ Works |
| `G31 Y[#15*2]` | macro_cam12.nc | 45 | ✅ Works |
| `G31 Y[#15*-2]` | macro_cam12.nc | 64 | ✅ Works |
| `X-#71Y-#72Z-#73...` | macro_DA.nc | 158 | ✅ Works |
| `G0 Z-#190` | macro_Thread_milling.nc | 105 | ✅ Works |
| `G1Z-#18F#28` | macro_Adaptive_Pocket.nc | 51 | ✅ Works |

**NOT found in any community macro:**
- `G31 Y+#1` (positive sign before variable)
- `G0 X+#2` (positive sign before variable)
